message.content:
Research configuration inheritance and import patterns in modern tooling.

Focus on:
1. ESLint's extends system:
   - extends: ["eslint:recommended", "./other-config"]
   - How plugins work
   - Override patterns

2. TypeScript's extends:
   - tsconfig.json extends
   - Path resolution

3. Babel's extends and presets

4. CSS @import and cascade

5. Docker's multi-stage inheritance

6. Terraform modules

7. Kubernetes Kustomize overlays

Which system has:
- Best import/extend syntax?
- Clearest override semantics?
- Most flexible inheritance?

How could these patterns apply to "agent rules" that cascade through directories?

Return specific syntax examples and recommendations.

content.text: Vou pesquisar os padrões de herança e importação nas ferramentas modernas de configuração.

input.query: ESLint extends configuration inheritance 2025 flat config system
TypeScript tsconfig extends path resolution inheritance 2025

Here are the search results for "Kubernetes Kustomize overlays inheritance patterns 2025":

## Kustomize Overlays Inheritance Patterns

### Core Concepts: Base and Overlays

Kustomize has two key concepts, Base and Overlays. With Kustomize you can reuse the base files (common YAMLs) across all environments and overlay (patches) specifications for each of those environments.(no content)Kustomize's base-and-overlay model eliminates the need to copy YAML across environments. A base defines the canonical, environment-agnostic configuration for an application. Overlays layer on only what differs—replica counts, resource limits, image tags, or configuration values—for development, staging, and production.

### Typical Project Structure

Links: [{"title":"TypeScript: TSConfig Option: extends","url":"https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig/extends.html"},{"title":"TypeScript: TSConfig Reference - Docs on every TSConfig option","url":"https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig/"},{"title":"Resolve tsconfig.json `extends` path using node_modules resolution logic · Issue #18865 · microsoft/TypeScript","url":"https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/18865"},{"title":"Add a option of type inheritance from extended tsconfig · Issue #57616 · microsoft/TypeScript","url":"https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/57616"},{"title":"tsconfig.json extends field node-style resolution broken · Issue #43245 · microsoft/TypeScript","url":"https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/43245"},{"title":"Deep Dive Into Extending tsconfig.json","url":"https://echobind.com/post/deep-dive-into-extending-tsconfig-json"},{"title":"Mastering TypeScript Config with Extends: A Comprehensive Guide","url":"https://www.webdevtutor.net/blog/typescript-tsconfig-extends"},{"title":"Everything You Need to Know About \"tsconfig.json\" in TypeScript","url":"https://overctrl.com/everything-you-need-to-know-about-tsconfig-json-in-typescript/"},{"title":"Configuration Inheritance · Issue #9876 · microsoft/TypeScript","url":"https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/9876"},{"title":"tsconfig.json · TypeScript","url":"https://typescript-site-76.ortam.vercel.app/docs/handbook/tsconfig-json.html"}]

Here are the search results for TypeScript tsconfig extends path resolution inheritance:

## Key Findings

### How `extends` Works in tsconfig.json

The value of `extends` is a string which contains a path to another configuration file to inherit from. The path may use Node.js style resolution. The configuration from the base file are loaded first, then overridden by those in the inheriting config file.

### Path Resolution Behavior

The problem is the paths in a tsconfig.json are relative to the folder that that specific tsconfig.json file is in, and not the tsconfig.json doing the extending.(no content)


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on my search, here's what I found about ESLint's `extends` configuration inheritance in the 2025 flat config system:

## ESLint Flat Config `extends` Feature (2025)

Published March 7, 2025, ESLint announced that eslint.config.js files can now use `extends` to simplify configuration.

### Background

When ESLint v9.0.0 was released in April 2024, the new configuration system was enabled by default.(no content)
